body_txt: Basically, it's all about insults which are... creative. Not so much like You Fight Like a Cow . These are more serious, but at the same time almost poetic, and often Sophisticated as Hell . Like these Arabian insults: Expect any person throwing out these to be very erudite, but by no means a pushover. If both opponents do this, Volleying Insults is likely to turn into a verbal Lensman Arms Race . On the other hand, if they receive less sophisticated insults, they'll likely react with disappointment , more than anger. As implied earlier, the Arab world really loves these insults. In Western works, it's not uncommon for any portrayal of a Middle-Eastern culture, whether it's realistic, orientalized , or fantastic but with Arabic inspirations , to have at least one native as an expert verbal attacker. Compare Cut His Heart Out with a Spoon , which is about threats instead. Compare and contrast Curse of The Ancients : it uses Antiquated Linguistics like many Flowery Insults, but are not necessarily more articulate or creative than normal insults, and the humor derives more on the speaker being portrayed as a crotchety old geezer, as opposed to an educated insult-slinger. Contrast Big, Stupid Doodoo-Head .
